creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
For reference, the i386 problem is an effect of an ancient GCC bug. See http://lists.llvm.org/pipermail/lldb-dev/2014-January/003096.html for a description of the issue.

It looks like this hacky patch might fix it:

--- a/contrib/llvm/projects/libunwind/include/libunwind.h
+++ b/contrib/llvm/projects/libunwind/include/libunwind.h
@@ -151,8 +151,8 @@ enum {
   UNW_X86_ECX = 1,
   UNW_X86_EDX = 2,
   UNW_X86_EBX = 3,
-  UNW_X86_EBP = 4,
-  UNW_X86_ESP = 5,
+  UNW_X86_ESP = 4,
+  UNW_X86_EBP = 5,
   UNW_X86_ESI = 6,
   UNW_X86_EDI = 7
 };

I need to do a little more research first for a fix, but will then follow-up with a request for another i386 exp-run.
